codeReview.summary: The implementation matches the reviewed authorization intent. `AdminUserService` now distinguishes between update operations that should keep returning `403` on out-of-scope targets and auth-sensitive user-control operations that should mask foreign-tenant targets as the existing `400 User not found` validation contract, while still logging internal `ACCESS_DENIED` audit evidence before returning the masked response. In parallel, `RoleController` now uses `@roleService.canManageSharedRoleMutation(authentication, #request.name())`, and the new guard method audits and blocks shared/system-role mutations at the controller boundary so tenant admins no longer reach later service denials for reviewed role-management cases. Targeted integration and contract coverage back both changes without widening the API surface.

summary: PASS. Foreign tenant-admin targets are externally indistinguishable from missing ids on the reviewed auth-sensitive endpoints, denial auditing remains intact, and controller/service authorization for role mutation is aligned at the intended boundary.
